generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor for precise angular measurement. Provides time-series measurements via MQTT streaming."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty in the physical system. Streams time-series data via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series angular position data from the motor encoder transmitted via MQTT protocol for real-time acquisition."
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series angular position data from the secondary encoder (post-gearbox) transmitted via MQTT protocol for real-time acquisition."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionShadowModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow artifact representing the 1:1 mapping between the physical first vertical robot axis and its digital twin. Aggregates contextual data traces for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol, SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance RobotAxisFunctionalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) representing the functional architecture of the first vertical robot axis, including component interfaces and connectors."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework, MontiArcFramework
]

instance RobotAxisLogicalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture description defining component-connector composition for the first vertical robot axis, including automata-based behavior modeling."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcFramework, SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance FrictionBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machine model representing dynamic friction compensation states for the robot axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcFramework, SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance RobotAxisSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for physics-based simulation of friction dynamics and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance RobotAxisControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "C++/Java implementation of control algorithms for friction compensation and optimization recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CppJavaImplementation
]

instance RobotAxisContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces (torque, temperature, angular velocity) from the physical system for Digital Shadow purposes."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol, DigitalShadowReferenceModel
]

instance RobotAxisFunctionalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral data derived from SysML IBDs and state machines representing the robot axis components and interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotAxisFunctionalArchitecture
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingFramework
]

instance RobotAxisLogicalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Component-connector architecture data from MontiArc defining interfaces and composition for the robot axis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotAxisLogicalArchitecture
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcFramework
]

instance RobotAxisBehavioralData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavior data from MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machines representing friction compensation states."
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionBehavioralModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcFramework
]

instance RobotAxisSimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation results from Simulink for friction dynamics and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotAxisSimulationModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance RobotAxisControlData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed control signals and optimization recommendations generated by C++/Java algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotAxisControlAlgorithm
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CppJavaImplementation
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT communication protocol enabling real-time data exchange between physical sensors and Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlAndOptimizationService
]

instance SysMLModelingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7+ framework for multi-level abstraction modeling (requirements, functional/logical architecture) using IBDs and state machines."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService, VisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc architecture description language for component-connector modeling and automata-based behavior representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService, SimulationAndAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink engine for physics-based simulation of friction dynamics and co-simulation with ADL models."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAndAnalysisService
]

instance CppJavaImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++/Java implementation framework for control algorithms, data processing pipelines, and active Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAndOptimizationService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UML class diagram-based reference model for aggregating contextual data traces (torque, temperature) into Digital Shadow artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized data exchange framework enabling interoperability between Digital Twin and industrial systems (PLCs, MES)."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive collection of contextual data traces (sensor data, operational logs) from the physical system, aggregated into Digital Shadow artifacts for real-time state observation."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotAxisStateInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of architecture description languages (ADLs) and state machines to model component interfaces, connectors, and dynamic behavior with multi-perspective validation."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobotAxisArchitectureInsight, RobotAxisBehaviorInsight
]

instance SimulationAndAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Use of simulation tools to predict system behavior, validate designs, and optimize performance through what-if scenarios and digital experimentation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionPredictionInsight, ControlValidationInsight
]

instance ControlAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active components interpreting processed data to generate control signals or optimization recommendations for autonomous decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction, OperationalEfficiencyAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-notation model integration (SysML IBDs, ADL diagrams) providing unified views for stakeholders with cross-tool interoperability."
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedSystemViewInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RobotAxisStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time state observation of the first vertical robot axis through aggregated sensor data traces."
]

instance RobotAxisArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-perspective validation of functional and logical architecture alignment for the robot axis."
]

instance RobotAxisBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavior representation through state machines and automata for friction compensation states."
]

instance FrictionPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted friction behavior under various operational conditions via physics-based simulation."
]

instance ControlValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of control algorithms through co-simulation with ADL and simulation models."
]

instance UnifiedSystemViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Cross-tool integrated visualization combining SysML, ADL diagrams, and simulation outputs for decision support."
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of control signals to compensate for friction-induced uncertainties in the robot axis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalEfficiencyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations for improving operational efficiency based on simulation and real-time data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor paired with gear transmission systems for actuation, controlled via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox providing gear transmission for the permanent magnet synchronous motor, with constrained actuation ranges based on gearbox ratios."
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller enabling individual axis control with configurable inputs for position, speed, or acceleration, with closed-loop feedback mechanisms for safety constraints."
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first vertical axis, including mechanical components and control systems."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, CycloidalGearbox, SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, utilizing **SysML** for system modeling across multiple abstraction layers (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). The architecture employs **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** to define components, interfaces, and interconnections, while **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** (v1.7+) are used for behavioral modeling. Integration with tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation environments (e.g., [29], [30]) enables detailed behavioral analysis. The system is designed to integrate heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives, with a focus on **component-based composition** and **interoperability** across modeling notations and tools. No explicit mention of dynamic orchestration or real-time coordination mechanisms is provided."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **fidelity requirements** through **validation and verification (V&V) methods**, primarily by comparing **simulated data** against **measured data** to quantify deviations. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via the **sensor arrangement**, which minimizes backlash and positional errors. **Error detection** is implicitly supported by the **state machine descriptions** in **SysML v1.7+** and **MontiArc Automaton**, though explicit **quality assurance processes** (e.g., formal methods) are not detailed in the provided documents. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** emphasizes **data aggregation and abstraction** to ensure contextual accuracy, but no explicit **error correction mechanisms** or **formal validation protocols** (e.g., model checking) are referenced."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow system leverages **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, utilizing tools such as **SysML** for architecture modeling and **Simulink** for behavioral simulation. The deployment integrates **heterogeneous stakeholders and modeling tools**, but no explicit details are provided on **hosting platforms** (cloud/edge/on-premise), **computational/storage requirements**, **scalability mechanisms**, or **availability/reliability assurances**. The system’s deployment is implicitly tied to the MBSE workflow, which may involve multiple modeling notations and tools but lacks infrastructure-specific details."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems via **MQTT** for streaming sensor data from the physical robot axis to the digital shadow. The document does not specify additional protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST) or middleware requirements, but the use of MQTT implies lightweight, publish-subscribe-based data exchange. Interoperability is achieved through **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and tool integration (e.g., Simulink), ensuring compatibility with enterprise systems or other Digital Twins via standardized modeling languages. No explicit adapters or middleware layers are mentioned beyond the MQTT-based data streaming."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** and **UML (Unified Modeling Language)** for modeling system architecture and behavior. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (as per Kritzinger et al.) aligns with **manufacturing-specific digital twin concepts**, though no explicit **industry standards (e.g., IEC 62443, ISO/IEC 25010)** or **certification frameworks** are mentioned. The document does not reference **RAMI 4.0** or **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**; these are not supported by the provided text. **Interoperability** is implied through the integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., Simulink, ADLs), but no specific compliance frameworks or protocols are detailed."
]
